message:
fix(fp-io): list_foreign_keys resolves implicit-PK references (br-cs1r / fd90.44)

Self-review of fd90.29 surfaced a real defect: when the user
declares FOREIGN KEY (col) REFERENCES parent (no column list),
SQLite's PRAGMA foreign_key_list returns NULL for the 'to' column.
Our prior implementation skipped the entire FK group via the
'columns.len() != referenced_columns.len()' guard, leaving callers
blind to a real foreign key. SQLAlchemy.Inspector resolves these
by looking up the parent table's primary key.

Fix: change the per-group resolution to a 3-way branch:
  1. all to_cols are None -> implicit PK reference. Look up parent's
     primary_key_columns (single round-trip via the existing trait
     method default impl). If parent's PK shape matches the FK
     column count, use it; otherwise skip (cannot fabricate).
  2. all to_cols are Some -> fully explicit, unchanged behavior.
  3. mixed Some/None -> SQLite shouldn't produce this, but skip
     defensively rather than mispairing.

Test additions:
- list_sql_foreign_keys_resolves_implicit_pk_single_column:
  REFERENCES parent (no col) resolves to parent's pid.
- list_sql_foreign_keys_resolves_implicit_pk_composite: composite
  FK with implicit reference resolves to parent's composite PK in
  declaration order.
- list_sql_foreign_keys_explicit_columns_unchanged: existing
  behavior preserved when columns are explicit.

414 fp-io tests pass; clippy -D warnings clean across fp-io +
umbrella; ubs Critical=0.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
